call_digest.call_type: Coaching / Spiritual Warfare Strategy - Number Two
main_themes: Spiritual Warfare: Body-slamming thoughts and biochemical priming
Pain as a Gift: The 'Spiritual Gym' framework
The 'Betrayal of the Future Wife': The cost of open doors
emotional_peaks: Arno's raw admission: 'My heart hurts some days.'
Andre's firm pivot from passive prayer to aggressive boundary setting (body-slamming Satan).
top_objections: Objection: 'I'm reading my Bible and praying, why is it still hitting me?'
Objection: 'Maybe I should pray for restoration?' (Lingering in the 'what-if' door)
top_truth_flips: Counting days is for people planning to return; identity is for people who have already left.
Allowing thoughts of an ex is a direct attack on your future wife's safety.
Spiritual pain isn't a problem to be removed; it's the weight that builds the muscle.

nuggets.idea_one_liner: Counting the days you've 'lasted' proves you're still the person who wants to go back.
nuggets.why_it_hits: Exposes the white-knuckle fallacy. If you're on Day 50, you're just a person who hasn't failed for 50 days, rather than a person who has actually moved on.
nuggets.proof_quote: Why are you counting? So that you can tell people how long you lasted this time? No. If you want to change, what you do is you change your identity.

value_equation.dream_outcome: Freedom from the urge to contact an ex.
numerator_notes: Highlight certainty of a new identity versus the fragility of a streak.
denominator_notes: Reduce effort by showing that 'being' is easier than 'fighting'.

hook_angles: Stop counting days. Start changing who you are.
Why your '100 days clean' streak is actually keeping you addicted.
The day you stop counting is the day you finally become a new man.

nuggets.idea_one_liner: You don't just pray about bad thoughts; you body-slam them.
nuggets.why_it_hits: It weaponizes the passive client. Turns 'suffering through' thoughts into an active spiritual combat role.
nuggets.proof_quote: Do you grab Satan by the throat and slam him on the ground? ... Today, I'm gonna body slam Satan when he tries to make me think about things.

numerator_notes: Promise power over your own mind.
denominator_notes: Provide an instant response to a trigger.

hook_angles: Why your 'gentle' prayers aren't stopping your anxiety.
The spiritual body-slam: how to treat intrusive thoughts.

nuggets.idea_one_liner: You can use spiritual pain to get 'buff' or stay 'skinny fat' at the bar of comfort.
nuggets.why_it_hits: It makes pain desirable via a masculine framework. Using gym progress to explain spiritual growth.
nuggets.proof_quote: If you want to be buff, it's a gift [pain]. ... Some people see that pain as a problem, and some people see that pain as an opportunity. ... As a result of them working out more, they start looking better and gaining muscle.

numerator_notes: Magnify the outcome of Christ-like strength.
denominator_notes: Show the pain is the mechanism of gain.

hook_angles: Why your heartbreak is actually your spiritual gym session.

objection_bank.objection_text: Maybe I should pray for restoration of the relationship?
objection_bank.underlying_belief: I can negotiate with God to get back the comfort I lost.
objection_bank.underlying_fear: God's better plan isn't actually better than the ex I know.
objection_bank.counter_truth_angle: Asking for 'comfort' when God is training you for 'strength' is choosing Judas's coin over the Cross.
objection_bank.transcript_proof_quote: So you asked God, God, to get Jack, do I have to go to the gym or can I just go to the bar instead?

top_5_nuggets.one_liner: Pain is the equipment, not the problem.
Streaks are for losers; Identity is for winners.

top_5_vsl_objections.why: Exposes the hidden selfishness in avoiding the spiritual gym.

creator_notes.tone: Direct, blunt, and aggressively protective of the future. (Reprocessed Run Number Two).
